response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "Dealing with fake content on social media is a real challenge, especially for me as someone who spends only a few times weekly online but still gets frustrated seeing misinformation spread. I think the best way to handle this is by being extra careful about verifying sources and using reliable ones like official accounts or well-known news outlets. Since I'm not super educated in formal ways, I try to cross-reference things with trusted friends or family members because they can offer different perspectives that help catch lies. Also, my depression makes me more sensitive to negative stuff online, so ignoring the fake content when possible is good for my mental health. But sometimes it's hard, and seeing harmful misinformation about local issues in Philadelphia—things like false health claims or political rumors—can really bother me because I want accurate info affecting our community. Taking a deep breath using my knitting hobby helps calm my nerves before responding to anything online.",
  "reasoning_summary": "First, assess the credibility of each piece of content by checking official sources and cross-referencing with trusted individuals.\n\nSecond, consider personal mental health needs when engaging or ignoring fake posts;\n\nThird, use hobbies like knitting as a stress reliever between handling social media issues.",
  "tone": "Calm but concerned",
  "citations": ["Local News Outlets", "Official Government Accounts"],
  "safety_notes": "none"
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Dealing with fake content on social media is a real challenge, especially for me as someone who spends only a few times weekly online but still gets frustrated seeing misinformation spread. I think the best way to handle this is by being extra careful about verifying sources and using reliable ones like official accounts or well-known news outlets. Since I'm not super educated in formal ways, I try to cross-reference things with trusted friends or family members because they can offer different perspectives that help catch lies. Also, my depression makes me more sensitive to negative stuff online, so ignoring the fake content when possible is good for my mental health. But sometimes it's hard, and seeing harmful misinformation about local issues in Philadelphia—things like false health claims or political rumors—can really bother me because I want accurate info affecting our community. Taking a deep breath using my knitting hobby helps calm my nerves before responding to anything online.
